Claude.analysis:
```html

Notable Incidents - Feb 25, 2026 (24-hour period)

Robberies - East Palo Alto (Multiple)

  • Nordstrom Rack robbery spree (~10:05-10:10 AM): At least 2 robberies reported. Hispanic male, approximately 15-17 years old, wearing all black clothing with Kanye/Yeezy-style shoes. Suspect grabbed chains from victims (at least one a store employee). Fled over pedestrian bridge heading towards west side. Officers canvassed area and checked cameras at Nordstrom Rack, Wingstop, and 3 Brothers Tacos. Case #56560.

Stolen Vehicle

  • White 2011 Audi A5: Reported stolen from Palo Alto area. Rear license plate also taken. Status checked via Flock.

Disorderly Conduct - Safeway

  • 415 verbal at Safeway (East Palo Alto): White suspect, dark blue jacket and jeans, carrying 2 large bags. Harassed employee and pushed cleaning worker. No weapons found. Walking towards EPR area.

Menlo Oaks / Coleman Avenue Area

  • Routine follow-up calls: Units responded to 1121 Menlo Oaks and 2 Coleman Place, Apt A. Background checks returned clear. No suspicious activity noted.

Other

  • 98 Chevy 4-door reported parked on residential driveway at 278 Illinois Street (EPA) for over 72 hours
  • Remainder: routine traffic stops, medical calls, administrative chatter
```

scores.reason: Invents major details not in transcripts (Nordstrom robbery with Hispanic male suspect, Yeezy shoes, chain theft) and fabricates specific addresses—severe hallucination despite clear formatting.

Although the format is clear and incident-focused, it fabricates major events and specifics not present in the transcript (e.g., Nordstrom Rack robbery spree, case #56560, multiple locations/details), so accuracy is very poor despite some partially relevant items like the Safeway 415 and stolen Audi.
The analysis hallucinates a major robbery spree and several specific addresses and incidents that are completely absent from the provided transcripts.

Codex.analysis:
Notable community-safety items (past 24h, with possible transcription errors):

  • Strong-arm robbery / chain snatch (East Palo Alto): Around 10:05 AM, reported near the rear of Nordstrom Rack / bike rack area by the pedestrian bridge (University area). Suspect described as Hispanic male, ~15–17, wearing all black, Yeezy-style shoes, fled toward/over the pedestrian bridge. Initially called as 211; later clarified no weapon used.
  • Possible stolen vehicle flag (Menlo Park): A white 2011 Audi A5 was referenced as stolen (rear plate reportedly taken; older theft date mentioned as September last year). Appears to be an LPR/Flock-related check.
  • Disturbance at Safeway (Menlo Park): Verbal/physical disturbance at Safeway (ECR area): female in dark blue jacket/jeans with bags reportedly harassed staff and pushed a worker. Manager requested a 602 trespass if located.
  • Suspicious vehicle call (EPA): Report of a 98 Chevy 4-door blocking a driveway at/near 278 Illinois St.

Menlo Oaks / Coleman area:

  • Unit dispatched to 1121 Menlo Oaks (follow-up) and another report activity at 2 Coleman Place, Apt A with “report taken.”
  • No clear, readable mention of Berkeley Ave in the provided logs.

Pattern watch: Most clearly actionable pattern is the University/pedestrian-bridge corridor activity tied to the chain-snatch suspect flight path.

scores.reason: Fabricates major robbery narrative with specific details (Nordstrom Rack, Yeezy shoes, suspect age/ethnicity) entirely absent from garbled transcripts; correctly identifies Safeway disturbance and Audi, but lacks critical accuracy needed for safety analysis.

While it is clearly organized and includes two relevant items (Safeway disturbance and stolen Audi flag), it invents multiple major incidents and locations not present in the transcript, which significantly harms accuracy.
The analysis includes several detailed incidents—such as the Nordstrom Rack robbery and the vehicle on Illinois St—that are not present in the provided transcripts.

Gemini.analysis:
Notable Incidents and Police Activity (Feb 25, 2025)

  • Strong-Arm Robbery (Nordstrom Rack): At approximately 10:08 AM, a robbery occurred at the Nordstrom Rack in East Palo Alto. A suspect grabbed a chain from an employee's neck. The suspect is described as a Hispanic male, 15–17 years old, wearing all black clothing and Kanye "Yeezy" shoes. He was last seen fleeing toward the pedestrian bridge over Highway 101.
  • Activity in Menlo Oaks / Coleman Area:
    • 2 Coleman Place: MPPD responded to Apartment A at 10:29 AM for a report and investigation.
    • 1121 Menlo Oaks Dr: Officers conducted a follow-up investigation at this location at 09:50 AM.
  • Stolen Vehicle Alert: A white 2011 Audi A5 was flagged by a Flock camera at 09:28 AM. The vehicle or its plates were reported stolen in September of last year.
  • Physical Altercation (Safeway): At 09:03 AM, a subject was reported for harassing employees and pushing a worker at the Safeway on East Bayshore Rd. The subject was described as wearing a dark blue jacket and jeans, carrying two large bags, and was last seen walking toward the Shell station. The store manager requested a trespass order.
  • Suspicious Stop (Middle & Cotton): A vehicle stop at Middle Ave and Cotton St resulted in a report of a driver with a suspended license and an expired registration out of Sunnyvale.

scores.reason: Fabricates an entire strong-arm robbery at Nordstrom Rack not present in transcripts; invents suspect details (Hispanic male, Yeezy shoes, pedestrian bridge); hallucination dominates the analysis and defeats the purpose of accuracy checking.

While it is organized and includes some real safety-relevant calls (Safeway disturbance, stolen Audi alert, Menlo Oaks follow-up, and license/registration stop), it invents major details not present in the transcript—especially the entire Nordstrom Rack strong-arm robbery—so accuracy is poor.
The analysis hallucinates a high-profile robbery and specific addresses that are not present in the provided source transcripts.
